### 1. Organizing Instruction and Study to Improve Student Learning

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/1"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [learning-engineering, cognitive-science, k-12, math-strategies, formative-assessment]
```

Published September 2007. Panel: Harold Pashler (chair, UC San Diego), Patrice M. Bain, Brian A. Bottge, Arthur Graesser, Kenneth Koedinger (CMU), Mark McDaniel, Janet Metcalfe. Seven recommendations for using learning and study time effectively in subjects requiring content mastery (science, social studies, mathematics): spacing content review, alternating worked examples with practice problems, combining visual and verbal instruction, linking abstract and concrete concepts, strategic use of quizzes, efficient study time allocation, and deep explanatory questioning.

---

### 2. Using Student Achievement Data to Support Instructional Decision Making

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/12"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [learning-engineering, formative-assessment, k-12, data-sharing, professional-development]
```

Published September 2009. Panel: Laura Hamilton (chair, RAND Corporation), Richard Halverson, Sharnell S. Jackson, Ellen Mandinach, Jonathan A. Supovitz, Jeffrey C. Wayman. Five recommendations for leveraging student data to monitor academic progress and evaluate teaching: establishing schoolwide data systems, fostering a data-informed culture, integrating data review into instructional improvement cycles, teaching students to interpret their own performance data for goal-setting, and implementing district-level evidence infrastructure.

---

### 3. Improving Mathematical Problem Solving in Grades 4 Through 8

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/16"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, math-word-problems, math-strategies, learning-engineering]
```

Published May 2012, revised October 2018. Panel: John Woodward (chair), Sybilla Beckmann, Mark Driscoll, Megan Franke, Patricia Herzig, Asha Jitendra, Kenneth R. Koedinger (CMU), Philip Ogbuehi. Prepared by Mathematica. Five recommendations for teachers, math coaches, and curriculum developers targeting grades 4–8: preparing problems for whole-class instruction, supporting student self-monitoring and reflection, teaching visual representation, exposing students to multiple solution strategies, and helping students recognize mathematical concepts and notation.

---

### 4. Teaching Math to Young Children

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/18"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, early-childhood, prekindergarten, k-12, learning-engineering]
```

Published November 2013. Panel: Douglas Frye (chair), Arthur J. Baroody, Margaret Burchinal, Sharon M. Carver, Nancy C. Jordan, Judy McDowell. Five recommendations for preschool, prekindergarten, and kindergarten educators: teaching number and operations using developmental progression (Moderate Evidence), teaching geometry and patterns developmentally (Minimal Evidence), using progress monitoring aligned to individual child knowledge (Minimal Evidence), teaching mathematical perspectives on everyday situations (Minimal Evidence), and dedicating daily math time integrated throughout instruction (Minimal Evidence). Each recommendation includes implementation steps and solutions for common obstacles.

---

### 5. Teaching Academic Content and Literacy to English Learners in Elementary and Middle School

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/19"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [english-learners, k-12, learning-engineering, professional-development]
```

Published April 2014. Panel: Scott Baker (chair), Esther Geva, Michael J. Kieffer, Nonie Lesaux, Sylvia Linan-Thompson, Joan Morris, C. Patrick Proctor, Randi Russell. Research staff: Joseph Dimino, Kelly Haymond, Russell Gersten, Madhavi Jayanthi, Rebecca Newman-Gonchar. Four recommendations for elementary and middle school educators: intensive academic vocabulary instruction using varied activities, integration of oral and written English instruction into content areas, structured opportunities for written language development, and small-group interventions for struggling learners. Includes examples aligned to Common Core standards.

---

### 6. Teaching Strategies for Improving Algebra Knowledge in Middle and High School Students

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/20"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, math-strategies, learning-engineering]
```

Published April 2015, revised January 2019. Panel: Jon R. Star (chair, Harvard University), Anne Foegen, Matthew R. Larson, William G. McCallum, Jane Porath, Rose Mary Zbiek. Three recommendations for middle and high school algebra educators: analyzing algebraic reasoning, utilizing structural representations, and selecting alternative problem-solving approaches. Each recommendation includes implementation steps and common roadblocks.

---

### 7. Using Technology to Support Postsecondary Student Learning

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/25"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, digital-learning-platforms, personalized-learning, learning-engineering]
```

Published May 2019. Authors: Sarah Costelloe, Kristen Cummings, Brian Freeman, Allan Porowski, Sandra Jo Wilson, Michael Frye. Five recommendations for higher education instructors, instructional designers, and administrators: use of communication tools, digital resources, self-regulated learning strategies, performance feedback, and simulation technologies for problem-solving. Targets postsecondary settings.

---

### 8. Assisting Students Struggling with Mathematics: Intervention in the Elementary Grades

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/26"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, math-strategies, learning-engineering, intelligent-tutoring]
```

Published March 2021. Panel: Lynn S. Fuchs (chair, Vanderbilt), Nicole Bucka, Ben Clarke, Barbara Dougherty, Nancy C. Jordan, Karen S. Karp, John Woodward. Six recommendations for grades K–6, all with strong evidence ratings: systematic instruction, mathematical language instruction, use of concrete representations, number line application, word problem instruction, and timed activities to build fluency. Covers both general student populations and children with disabilities.

---

### 9. Preparing Young Children for School

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/30"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [early-childhood, prekindergarten, k-12, learning-engineering]
```

Published August 2022. Panel: Margaret Burchinal (chair, University of Virginia), Karen Bierman, Jorge Gonzalez, Kimberly Nelson, Megan McClelland, Jill Pentimonti, David Purpura, Jason Sachs, Julie Sarama, Elizabeth Schlesinger-Devlin, Julie Washington. Seven recommendations distilling contemporary early childhood and preschool research to help prepare young children to benefit from educational opportunities in school settings.

---

### 10. CAST Universal Design for Learning Guidelines 3.0

```yaml
url: "https://udlguidelines.cast.org"
type: framework
source: "CAST"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [learning-engineering, k-12, inclusive-design, cognitive-science]
```

Released July 30, 2024. Organization: CAST. An educational framework "based on scientific insights into how humans learn" designed as "a tool that can be applied in any discipline or domain to ensure that all learners are able to access and participate in meaningful, challenging learning opportunities." Version 3.0 specifically addresses "critical barriers rooted in biases and systems of exclusion for learners with and without disabilities." Three design principles: Engagement, Representation, and Action & Expression. Overarching goal: learner agency that is "purposeful & reflective, resourceful & authentic, strategic & action-oriented." Downloadable PDF available in 20+ languages. Audience: educators, curriculum developers, learning and instructional designers, researchers, parents.

---

### 11. Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence

```yaml
url: "https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/recommendation-ethics-artificial-intelligence?hub=195885"
type: framework
source: "UNESCO"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [ai-policy, ai-ethics, k-12, higher-ed]
```

Adopted 2021, published 2022. UNESCO's inaugural global standard on AI ethics, addressed to all 194 UNESCO member states. Establishes that "the protection of human rights and dignity is the cornerstone" of the framework. Covers transparency, fairness, and human oversight of AI systems across Policy Action Areas addressing data governance, environmental concerns, gender equity, education, research, health, and social wellbeing.

---

### 12. UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Students

```yaml
url: "https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/what-you-need-know-about-unescos-new-ai-competency-frameworks-students-and-teachers"
type: framework
source: "UNESCO"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [ai-policy, k-12, higher-ed, ai-ethics, llm-application]
```

Published 2024. Authors: Fengchun Miao, Kelly Shiohira, Natalie Lao. Four core competency areas for students (K-12 and higher education): "a human-centred mindset," "ethics of AI," "AI techniques and applications," and "AI system design." Direct PDF available in English, Arabic, French, Portuguese, Spanish, and Vietnamese at unesdoc.unesco.org (access from direct UNESDOC URL was blocked during audit; description sourced from UNESCO article page).

---

### 13. UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Teachers

```yaml
url: "https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/what-you-need-know-about-unescos-new-ai-competency-frameworks-students-and-teachers"
type: framework
source: "UNESCO"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [ai-policy, professional-development, k-12, higher-ed, ai-ethics, llm-application]
```

Published 2024. Authors: Fengchun Miao, Mutlu Cukurova. Five competency areas for teachers and educators: "a human-centred mindset," "ethics of AI," "AI foundations and applications," "AI pedagogy," and "AI for professional development." Direct PDF available in English, Arabic, French, Portuguese, Spanish, and Vietnamese at unesdoc.unesco.org (access from direct UNESDOC URL was blocked during audit; description sourced from UNESCO article page).

---

### 15. EMERALDS: Preparation for Success in Algebra

```yaml
url: "https://achievethecore.org/page/3367/preparation-for-success-in-algebra-exploring-math-education-relationships-by-analyzing-large-data-sets-emeralds"
type: report
source: "Carnegie Mellon University / Educational Testing Service"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
doi: null
license: null
tags: [math-education, k-12, longitudinal, math-strategies, learning-engineering, cmu-learnlab, carnegie-learning]
```

Published June 2021. Principal Investigator: David C. Geary (University of Missouri). Institutions: Human-Computer Interaction Institute at Carnegie Mellon University and Educational Testing Service. "One of the largest longitudinal assessments of what mathematics prepares students for success in Algebra" — uses anonymized large-scale data to identify mathematical competencies in earlier grades that form critical foundations for algebraic success, with particular focus on historically underserved student populations. Report hosted at Achieve the Core; full report linked from that page.

---

### 16. Lenses on Literature — Evidence for ESSA

```yaml
url: "https://evidenceforessa.org/program/lenses-on-literature/"
type: report
source: "Evidence for ESSA"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
doi: null
license: null
tags: [k-12, computer-assisted-learning, carnegie-learning, learning-engineering, formative-assessment]
```

Evidence review published at Evidence for ESSA (Johns Hopkins University). Carnegie Learning's Lenses on Literature (formerly Literacy Design Collaborative) earned a "Promising" (Tier 3) ESSA evidence rating. Instructional framework emphasizing daily, standards-driven reading, writing, and discourse organized around disciplinary literacy with complex texts. Primary study: 18,203 students across Kentucky and Pennsylvania (2012–2013). Effect size: +0.05 average; Kentucky: +0.06 (statistically significant); Pennsylvania: +0.03 (non-significant). Includes scaffolds for multilingual learners, embedded assessments using Stanford-aligned rubrics, and professional development with coaching and data analysis.

---

### 17. The State of Educational Data Mining in 2009: A Review and Future Visions

```yaml
url: "https://jedm.educationaldatamining.org/index.php/JEDM/article/view/8"
type: paper
source: "Journal of Educational Data Mining"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.3554657"
license: "CC BY-NC-ND 4.0"
tags: [learning-engineering, meta-analysis, intelligent-tutoring, computer-assisted-learning, jedm]
```

Published 2009. Authors: Ryan S.J.d. Baker (Worcester Polytechnic Institute), Kalina Yacef (University of Sydney). Journal of Educational Data Mining, Vol. 1, No. 1. Inaugural-issue review paper for the EDM field: examines the history and methodological evolution of educational data mining, comparing early approaches with 2008–2009 research. Identifies three field-wide trends: increased emphasis on prediction methods, emergence of discovery-with-models techniques, and declining focus on relationship mining. Catalogues the most-cited EDM papers published 1995–2005 and analyzes their influence within and beyond the field. Open access under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

---

### 18. Breaking With the Past: Embracing Digital Transformation in Education

```yaml
url: "https://digitalpromise.dspacedirect.org/items/6559b261-d6d7-4184-9116-16eb93a9c212"
type: report
source: "Digital Promise"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
doi: "10.51388/20.500.12265/176"
license: null
tags: [digital-learning-platforms, personalized-learning, learning-engineering, digital-promise]
```

Digital Promise report arguing for digital transformation of teaching and learning at scale across U.S. schools. Compares traditional one-size-fits-all instruction to learner-centered, personalized frameworks and examines how digital technologies can enable the transition—drawing on how other industries have used innovation to drive systemic change.

---

### 19. Computational Thinking for an Inclusive World

```yaml
url: "https://digitalpromise.dspacedirect.org/handle/20.500.12265/138"
type: report
source: "Digital Promise"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
doi: null
license: null
tags: [k-12, computer-assisted-learning, learning-engineering, digital-promise, inclusive-design]
```

Digital Promise report calling on educators to integrate computational thinking across PreK–12 disciplinary learning with an explicit equity focus, addressing persistent gaps in access to computing education for marginalized students. Argues that CT skills are important for all students—not only future computer science students—and issues two calls to action for designing inclusive computing learning opportunities.

---

### 20. Shifting Mindsets: Designing Lessons for Learner Variability

```yaml
url: "https://digitalpromise.dspacedirect.org/handle/20.500.12265/157"
type: report
source: "Digital Promise"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
doi: null
license: null
tags: [personalized-learning, professional-development, learning-engineering, digital-promise]
```

Digital Promise pilot study conducted with DonorsChoose examining whether teachers could use the Learner Variability Navigator (LVN)—a free, open-source web app curating whole-child research and evidence-based strategies—to customize instruction for diverse learners. Found that even brief use of the tool encouraged teachers to apply research-based strategies addressing learner variability across PreK–12 classrooms.

---

### 21. Assisting Students Struggling with Mathematics: Response to Intervention (RtI) for Elementary and Middle Schools

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/2"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, response-to-intervention, math-strategies, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published April 2009. Panel: Russell Gersten (chair, Instructional Research Group), Sybilla Beckmann, Benjamin Clarke, Anne Foegen, Laurel Marsh, Jon R. Star, Bradley Witzel. Eight recommendations for elementary and middle school math intervention using the Response to Intervention framework: screen all students and provide targeted support (Moderate), emphasize whole numbers K–5 and rational numbers grades 4–8 (Minimal), use explicit and systematic instruction (Strong), teach problem solving via common structural patterns (Strong), incorporate visual representations (Moderate), build fact fluency with approximately 10 minutes per session (Moderate), monitor student progress throughout intervention (Minimal), and include motivational strategies in secondary and tertiary intervention tiers (Minimal).

---

### 22. Assisting Students Struggling with Reading: Response to Intervention (RtI) and Multi-Tier Intervention in the Primary Grades

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/3"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published February 2009. Panel: Russell Gersten (chair), Donald Compton, Carol M. Connor, Joseph Dimino, Lana Santoro, Sylvia Linan-Thompson, W. David Tilly. Five recommendations for primary-grade reading intervention using a multi-tier approach: screen all students for reading problems at the beginning and middle of the year (Moderate), provide differentiated reading instruction based on current assessments (Minimal), deliver intensive systematic instruction on up to three foundational reading skills in small groups for below-benchmark students (Strong), monitor tier 2 student progress monthly (Minimal), and deliver daily intensive instruction to tier 3 students showing minimal progress (Minimal).

---

### 23. Reducing Behavior Problems in the Elementary School Classroom

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/4"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [sel, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published September 2008. Panel: Michael Epstein (chair, University of Nebraska, Lincoln), Marc Atkins, Douglas Cullinan, Krista Kutash, Robin Weaver. Five recommendations for elementary classroom behavior management: identify specific conditions triggering problem behavior (Moderate), modify the classroom environment to remove triggers (Strong), actively teach socially and behaviorally appropriate replacement skills (Strong), leverage relationships with colleagues, families, and behavioral experts (Moderate), and implement schoolwide prevention strategies requiring shared responsibility (Moderate).

---

### 24. Encouraging Girls in Math and Science

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/5"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published September 2007. Panel: Diane F. Halpern (chair, Claremont McKenna), Joshua Aronson (NYU), Nona Reimer, Sandra Simpkins (Arizona State), Jon R. Star (Harvard), Kathryn Wentzel (Maryland). Five recommendations for broadening girls' participation in STEM: teach that academic abilities are expandable and improvable (Moderate), provide prescriptive informational feedback about student performance (Moderate), introduce female role models in math and science fields (Minimal), create learning environments that build curiosity and avoid gender stereotypes (Moderate), and integrate spatial skills training into instruction (Minimal).

---

### 25. Effective Literacy and English Language Instruction for English Learners in the Elementary Grades

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/6"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [english-learners, literacy, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published December 2007. Panel: Russell Gersten (chair), Scott K. Baker, Penny Collins, Sylvia Linan-Thompson, Robin Scarcella, Timothy Shanahan. Five recommendations for literacy instruction with elementary English learners: screen for reading problems and monitor progress in English (Strong), provide intensive small-group reading interventions for at-risk students (Strong), deliver extensive and varied vocabulary instruction throughout the school day (Strong), make formal academic English development a key instructional goal beginning in primary grades (Minimal), and schedule regular peer-assisted learning for structured academic collaboration approximately 90 minutes weekly (Strong).

---

### 26. Turning Around Chronically Low-Performing Schools

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/7"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published May 2008. Panel: Rebecca Herman (chair, American Institutes for Research), Priscilla Dawson, Thomas Dee, Jay Greene, Rebecca Maynard, Sam Redding, Marlene Darwin. Four recommendations for school turnaround leaders: signal the need for dramatic change with leadership communicating urgency to staff and stakeholders; maintain a consistent focus on improving instruction by leveraging data to set goals and reassess learning continuously; make visible improvements early in the turnaround process to motivate staff and reduce resistance; and build a committed staff aligned with improvement objectives.

---

### 27. Improving Adolescent Literacy: Effective Classroom and Intervention Practices

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/8"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published August 2008. Panel: Michael L. Kamil (chair, Stanford), Geoffrey D. Borman, Janice Dole, Cathleen C. Kral, Terry Salinger, Joseph Torgesen. Five recommendations for grades 5–12 literacy: provide explicit vocabulary instruction (Strong), deliver direct and explicit comprehension strategy instruction (Strong), facilitate extended discussion of text meaning and interpretation (Moderate), increase student motivation and engagement with reading (Moderate), and provide intensive individualized reading interventions by trained specialists for struggling readers (Strong).

---

### 28. Structuring Out-of-School Time to Improve Academic Achievement

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/10"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, personalized-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published July 2009. Panel: Megan Beckett (chair, RAND), Geoffrey Borman, Jeffrey Capizzano, Danette Parsley, Steven Ross, Allen Schirm, Jessica Taylor. Five recommendations for administrators and educators designing out-of-school programs: align the OST program academically with the school day (Minimal), maximize student participation and attendance (Minimal), adapt instruction to individual and small group needs (Moderate), provide engaging learning experiences (Minimal), and assess program performance using results to continuously improve quality (Minimal).

---

### 29. Helping Students Navigate the Path to College: What High Schools Can Do

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/11"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, k-12, college-access, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published September 2009. Panel: William G. Tierney (chair, USC), Thomas Bailey (Columbia), Jill Constantine, Neal Finkelstein (WestEd), Nicole Farmer Hurd (National College Advising Corps). Five recommendations for high schools: provide college-preparatory coursework and ensure ninth graders understand college-readiness requirements; use ongoing assessments to monitor readiness and address skill gaps; foster college aspirations through supportive adult and peer networks; facilitate completion of essential college application steps (Moderate); and enhance financial literacy and support the financial aid application process (Moderate).

---

### 30. Improving Reading Comprehension in Kindergarten Through 3rd Grade

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/14"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, early-childhood, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published September 2010. Panel: Timothy Shanahan (chair, University of Chicago), Kim Callison, Christine Carriere, Nell K. Duke (Michigan State), P. David Pearson (UC-Berkeley), Christopher Schatschneider (Florida State/FCRR), Joseph Torgesen (Florida State/FCRR). Five recommendations for K–3 reading comprehension: train students to apply reading comprehension strategies (Strong), instruct students to leverage text structure for comprehension and retention (Moderate), facilitate substantive dialogue about textual meaning (Minimal), choose materials strategically to foster comprehension growth (Minimal), and establish an engaging and motivating context for reading instruction (Moderate).

---

### 31. Developing Effective Fractions Instruction for Kindergarten Through 8th Grade

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/15"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, math-strategies, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published September 2010. Panel: Robert Siegler (chair, Carnegie Mellon University), Thomas Carpenter, Francis Fennell, David Geary (University of Missouri), James Lewis, Yukari Okamoto, Laurie Thompson, Jonathan Wray. Five recommendations for fractions instruction grades K–8: build on students' informal understanding of sharing and proportionality; establish that fractions are numbers that expand the number system beyond whole numbers; support understanding of the reasoning behind fraction computation procedures; develop conceptual understanding of ratio, rate, and proportion before introducing cross-multiplication; and improve teachers' own fraction knowledge through professional development. Emphasizes number lines as a central teaching tool across grade levels.

---

### 32. Teaching Elementary School Students to Be Effective Writers

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/17"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, writing-instruction, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published June 2012, revised October 2018. Panel: Steve Graham (chair, Arizona State), Alisha Bollinger, Carol Booth Olson (UC Irvine), Catherine D'Aoust, Charles MacArthur (University of Delaware), Deborah McCutchen (University of Washington), Natalie Olinghouse (University of Connecticut). Four recommendations for grades 1–6 writing instruction: provide daily time for students to write (Minimal), teach students to use the writing process for a variety of purposes (Strong), teach fluency with handwriting, spelling, sentence construction, typing, and word processing (Moderate), and create an engaged community of writers (Minimal).

---

### 33. Foundational Skills to Support Reading for Understanding in Kindergarten Through 3rd Grade

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/21"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, early-childhood, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published July 2016, revised December 2019. Panel: Barbara Foorman (chair, Florida State/FCRR), Michael Coyne, Carolyn A. Denton, Joseph Dimino, Lynda Hayes, Laura Justice, Warnick Lewis, Richard Wagner (Florida State/FCRR). Four recommendations for K–3 foundational reading: teach academic language skills including inferential language and vocabulary (Minimal), develop phonological awareness and sound-letter links (Strong), teach decoding, word analysis, and written word recognition (Strong), and ensure each student reads connected text daily to build accuracy, fluency, and comprehension (Moderate).

---

### 34. Teaching Secondary Students to Write Effectively

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/22"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, writing-instruction, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published November 2016, revised December 2019. Panel: Steve Graham (chair, Arizona State), Jill Fitzgerald, Linda D. Friedrich (National Writing Project), Katie Greene, James S. Kim (Harvard), Carol Booth Olson (UC Irvine). Three recommendations for grades 6–12 writing instruction across all subject areas: explicitly teach appropriate writing strategies using a Model-Practice-Reflect instructional cycle (Strong), integrate writing and reading to emphasize key writing features (Moderate), and use assessments of student writing to inform instruction and feedback (Minimal).

---

### 35. Strategies for Postsecondary Students in Developmental Education

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/23"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, college-access, personalized-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published November 2016. Panel: Thomas Bailey (chair, Teachers College, Columbia), Joanne Bashford, Angela Boatman (Vanderbilt), John Squires (Southern Regional Education Board), Michael Weiss (MDRC). Six recommendations for college administrators, advisors, and faculty serving academically underprepared postsecondary students: use multiple measures to assess readiness and place students (Minimal), enhance advising through required or incentivized regular participation (Moderate), implement monetary performance incentives (Moderate), redesign developmental education through compression or mainstreaming (Minimal), teach self-regulated learning strategies (Minimal), and create comprehensive integrated support programs (Moderate).

---

### 36. Preventing Dropout in Secondary Schools

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/24"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, dropout-prevention, personalized-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published September 2017. Panel: Russell W. Rumberger (chair, UC Santa Barbara), Howard Addis, Elaine Allensworth (University of Chicago Consortium), Robert Balfanz (Johns Hopkins), Debra Duardo, Mark Dynarski. Updated version of the 2008 dropout prevention guide, incorporating nine additional years of research. Four recommendations for grades 6–12: monitor all students and proactively intervene on early signs of attendance, behavior, or academic problems (Minimal); deliver tailored support to students who have fallen behind and face substantial barriers (Moderate); involve students through educational offerings bridging learning to college and career while building resilience (Strong); and for schools with many at-risk students, establish small personalized learning communities for better tracking and assistance (Moderate).

---

### 37. Designing and Delivering Career Pathways at Community Colleges

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/27"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, career-readiness, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published March 2021. Panel: Hope Cotner (chair, CORD), Debra Bragg (University of Washington), Grant Goold, Eric Heiser, Darlene G. Miller (National Council for Workforce Education), Michelle Van Noy (Rutgers). Five recommendations for community college administrators, faculty, and staff: structure pathways enabling students to advance education, secure employment, and progress professionally (Moderate); provide contextualized basic skills training accelerating pathway entry and completion (Moderate); offer flexible schedules and instructional models boosting credential completion (Minimal); coordinate comprehensive student services to enhance credit accumulation (Moderate); and develop strategic employer and community partnerships preparing students for labor market success (Moderate).

---

### 38. Effective Advising for Postsecondary Students

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/28"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, college-access, professional-development, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published October 2021. Panel: Melinda Karp (chair, Phase Two Advisory), Brett McFarlane, Donna Linderman (CUNY), Joe O'Shea (Florida State), Lashawn Richburg-Hayes (Insight Policy Research), Sara Ackerson. Four recommendations for postsecondary advising: design comprehensive advising combining academic and non-academic supports (Moderate); build sustained, personalized advisor-student relationships throughout the college experience (Strong); employ mentoring and coaching to strengthen advising and support student achievement (Strong); and integrate positive incentives into advising structures to boost sustained student participation (Strong).

---

### 39. Providing Reading Interventions for Students in Grades 4–9

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/PracticeGuide/29"
type: framework
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

Published March 2022. Panel: Sharon Vaughn (chair, University of Texas at Austin), Michael J. Kieffer (NYU), Margaret McKeown (Pittsburgh), Deborah K. Reed, Michele Sanchez, Kimberly St. Martin (MiMTSS), Jade Wexler (Maryland). Four recommendations for grades 4–9 reading intervention: build students' decoding skills for complex multisyllabic words (Strong), provide purposeful fluency-building activities (Strong), implement interconnected comprehension practices covering vocabulary, question-and-answer activities, text-gist routines, and self-monitoring (Strong), and provide opportunities for students to practice with challenging stretch text (Moderate).

---

### 40. Knowledge is Power Program (KIPP) Charter Schools

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/688"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on KIPP, a nationwide network of free open-enrollment college-preparatory schools in under-resourced communities providing approximately 60% more instructional time than traditional public schools. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on mathematics and English language arts achievement, potentially positive effects on science and social studies achievement, and no discernible effects on student progression for grades 5–12.

---

### 41. Cognitive Tutor® Algebra I

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/655"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, intelligent-tutoring, computer-assisted-learning, learning-engineering, carnegie-learning, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Cognitive Tutor® Algebra I, published by Carnegie Learning — a math curriculum combining textbooks with adaptive intelligent tutoring software. Reviews 6 studies meeting standards out of 18 eligible. Found positive effects on algebra achievement (grades 8–PS, Tier 1) and no discernible effects on general mathematics achievement in later grades (9–10).

---

### 42. Intelligent Tutoring for Structure Strategy (ITSS)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/703"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, intelligent-tutoring, computer-assisted-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on ITSS, a web-based supplemental program teaching students to identify and use text structure patterns (comparison, cause-effect, sequence, description, problem-solution) to improve literacy comprehension and recall in grades K–8. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on comprehension (grades 4–7, Tier 1).

---

### 43. Open Learning Initiative (OLI)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/698"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, computer-assisted-learning, digital-learning-platforms, learning-engineering, cmu-learnlab, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Carnegie Mellon University's Open Learning Initiative, offering innovative online courses designed to create high-quality learning experiences and advance educational research. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found mixed effects on academic achievement and potentially positive effects on credit accumulation and persistence for postsecondary students.

---

### 44. DreamBox Learning

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/627"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, computer-assisted-learning, personalized-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on DreamBox Learning, a supplemental online adaptive mathematics program for grades K–5 that individualizes instruction by creating unique paths through the curriculum matching each student's level of comprehension and learning style. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement (K–1, Tier 3), based on a predominantly Hispanic, English learner, urban sample.

---

### 45. READ 180®

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/665"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, computer-assisted-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on READ 180®, a reading program combining online and direct instruction, student assessment, and teacher professional development for struggling readers 2+ years below grade level in grades 4–10. Reviews 9 studies meeting standards out of 39 eligible. Found positive effects on comprehension and general literacy achievement, potentially positive effects on reading fluency, and no discernible effects on alphabetics.

---

### 46. Achieve3000®

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/691"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, computer-assisted-learning, personalized-learning, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Achieve3000®, a supplemental online literacy program providing differentiated nonfiction reading content for grades preK–12, targeting phonemic awareness, phonics, fluency, comprehension, vocabulary, and writing. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension and general literacy achievement for adolescent readers (Tier 2).

---

### 47. Success for All®

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/672"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Success for All® (SFA®), a whole-school reform model integrating curriculum, school culture, family, and community supports, emphasizing phonics for beginning readers and comprehension for all students in grades K–4. Reviews 9 studies meeting standards out of 49 eligible. Found positive effects on alphabetics, potentially positive effects on reading fluency, and mixed effects on comprehension and general reading achievement.

---

### 48. Self-Regulated Strategy Development (SRSD)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/680"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, writing-instruction, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on SRSD, an intervention for grades 2–12 that improves academic skills through a six-step process teaching specific academic strategies and self-regulation, progressing from teacher-directed instruction to independent student application. Reviews 10 studies meeting standards out of 16 eligible. Based on single-case design studies, found potentially positive effects on writing achievement for students with specific learning disabilities (Tier 3).

---

### 49. Growth Mindset (Postsecondary)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/719"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, student-belonging, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report reviewing growth mindset programs at the postsecondary level — interventions encouraging students to view intelligence as adaptable and academic challenges as surmountable. Reviews 6 studies meeting standards out of 10 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement (Tier 2) and no discernible effects on college enrollment or progressing in college.

---

### 50. Accelerated Study in Associate Programs (ASAP)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/695"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, college-access, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on ASAP, a three-year program removing barriers to associate degree completion by providing financial, academic, and personal supports while requiring full-time enrollment. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found positive effects on access/enrollment, degree attainment, and credit accumulation/persistence (Tier 1 across all three outcome domains).

---

### 51. Dual Enrollment Programs

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/671"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, higher-ed, college-access, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on dual enrollment programs allowing high school students to take college courses and earn college credits, aimed at enhancing college access and degree completion for underrepresented students in grades 9–12. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 35 eligible. Found positive effects (Tier 1) on degree attainment, college access/enrollment, credit accumulation, high school completion, and high school academic achievement.

### 52. Head Start

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/636"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [early-childhood, prekindergarten, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Head Start, the national federally-funded program providing education, health, nutrition, family engagement, and social services to promote school readiness for children ages 3–4 from predominantly low-income families. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 40 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading achievement and no discernible effects on mathematics or social-emotional development.

### 53. Leveled Literacy Intervention

WWC Intervention Report on Leveled Literacy Intervention, a short-term supplementary small-group literacy program for grades K–2 struggling readers delivering explicit instruction in phonological awareness, phonics, fluency, vocabulary, comprehension, oral language, and writing. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 10 eligible. Found positive effects on general reading achievement (Tier 1), potentially positive effects on reading fluency, and no discernible effects on alphabetics.

### 54. Peer-Assisted Learning Strategies (PALS) — Beginning Reading

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/364"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on PALS, a peer-tutoring program for grades K–6 in which students take turns acting as tutor and tutee, coaching and correcting one another through literacy problems. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 13 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on alphabetics (K–1, Tier 2), no discernible effects on reading fluency, and mixed effects on comprehension.

### 55. Good Behavior Game

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/728"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, sel, school-discipline, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Good Behavior Game, a classroom management strategy for grades K–11 that organizes students into teams and rewards appropriate conduct to improve social skills and minimize disruptive behaviors. Reviews 16 studies meeting standards out of 31 eligible. Found positive effects on student behavior and teacher practice, potentially positive effects on writing conventions and writing productivity, and uncertain effects on academic achievement and school climate.


WWC Intervention Report on Everyday Mathematics®, a core PreK–6 curriculum emphasizing real-life problem solving, student communication of mathematical thinking, technology use, collaborative learning, and parent involvement. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 34 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement for grades 3–5.

### 57. Career Academies

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/70"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, career-readiness, dropout-prevention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Career Academies, school-within-school high school programs where students take integrated career and academic courses while gaining real work experience through employer partnerships. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 9 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on school completion and staying in school, and no discernible effects on academic progress.

### 58. Check & Connect

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/78"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, dropout-prevention, attendance, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Check & Connect, a dropout prevention strategy for grades 9–12 combining close monitoring of school performance with mentoring, case management, and individualized student supports. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found positive effects on staying in school, potentially positive effects on progressing in school, and no discernible effects on completing school for students with learning, behavioral, or emotional disabilities.

---

### 59. Teach for America (TFA)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/663"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, professional-development, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Teach for America, a highly selective alternative route to teacher certification placing non-traditionally trained teachers in high-need public schools with 5–7 weeks of summer training plus ongoing professional development and coaching. Reviews 7 studies meeting standards out of 24 eligible. Found positive effects on mathematics achievement, potentially positive effects on science achievement, and no discernible effects on English language arts or social studies for grades preK–12.

---

### 60. MyTeachingPartner-Secondary (MTP-S)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/635"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, professional-development, coaching, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on MTP-S, a web-based professional development program for grades 6–12 providing teachers access to a video library of high-quality teaching examples and individualized coaching approximately twice monthly, using the CLASS-S framework to define and observe effective teaching practice. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general academic achievement for middle and high school students (Tier 3).

---

### 61. Science Teachers Learning from Lesson Analysis (STeLLA)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/716"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, professional-development, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on STeLLA, a professional development program by BSCS Science Learning for grades 4–5 teachers that improves student science achievement by enhancing teachers' science content knowledge and ability to explain concepts, identify learning objectives, and engage students in scientific thinking. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on science achievement compared with another yearlong teacher PD program (Tier 3).

---

### 62. Dual Language Programs

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/726"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, english-learners, literacy, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on dual language programs providing content and literacy instruction through two languages (English and a partner language) in grades K–8, targeting academic achievement, bilingualism, biliteracy, and sociocultural competence. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on English literacy achievement (Tier 2) and uncertain effects on mathematics and science achievement.

### 63. Fraction Face-Off!

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/702"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [math-education, k-12, math-strategies, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Fraction Face-Off!, a supplemental math program for grade 4 students needing additional support with fraction problems, delivered individually or in small groups by teachers. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement, geometry and measurement, and number and operations (Tier 3), with a diverse sample (52% Black, 88% free/reduced-price lunch, 15% English learners).

---

### 64. Year Up

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/722"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, career-readiness, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Year Up, a postsecondary program providing six months of occupational and technical training in information technology and financial services followed by six-month internships, targeting youth in under-resourced communities. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on short-term earnings but no discernible effects on medium-term earnings, employment outcomes, or credential completion.

---

### 65. Project QUEST (Quality Employment through Skills Training)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/723"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, career-readiness, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Project QUEST, a postsecondary workforce program providing comprehensive support services to help participants complete community college occupational training, pass certification exams, and obtain well-paying jobs in targeted sectors. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards. Found positive effects on credential/certificate completion, potentially positive effects on credit accumulation, and no discernible effects on employment or earnings outcomes.

---

### 66. Integrated Basic Education and Skills Training (I-BEST)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/706"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, career-readiness, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on I-BEST, Washington State's program integrating basic skills and occupational training so students complete workforce training faster than traditional programs with wraparound supports. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on credential/certificate/license completion (Tier 1), potentially positive effects on short-term earnings and employment, and no discernible effects on credit accumulation.

### 67. Social Skills Training (Early Childhood, Children with Disabilities)

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/578"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [early-childhood, sel, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on social skills training for preschool children with disabilities — a collection of behavioral practices teaching age-appropriate communication, problem solving, decision making, self-management, and peer relations in early childhood education settings. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on social-emotional development and no discernible effects on cognition.

---

### 68. Repeated Reading

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/576"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Repeated Reading, an academic practice for grades 5–12 in which students read passages aloud multiple times with teacher support in a one-on-one setting to increase oral reading fluency. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 15 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading comprehension for students with learning disabilities (Tier 3) and no discernible effects on alphabetics, reading fluency, or general reading achievement.

---

### 69. Single Stop

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/709"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [higher-ed, college-access, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Single Stop, a community college program connecting students and their families to public benefits through screening, application support, and wraparound services delivered at on-campus locations. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on academic achievement and progressing in college (Tier 2) for a predominantly Hispanic community college student population.

---

### 70. Reading Recovery®

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/730"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Reading Recovery®, a one-on-one tutoring intervention for grade 1 students with low literacy achievement delivering 30-minute daily sessions over 12–20 weeks, tailoring instruction in phonemic awareness, phonics, vocabulary, fluency, comprehension, writing, and oral language. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 20 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on literacy achievement, writing productivity, and receptive communication skills, with uncertain long-term effects on mathematics and academic achievement.

---

### 71. Reading Apprenticeship®

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/727"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, professional-development, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Reading Apprenticeship®, a professional development program for grades 7–12 teachers that improves student literacy and also targets social-emotional outcomes including belonging, growth mindset, and self-efficacy. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 9 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on science achievement and grade point average for grade 9 students (Tier 2) and uncertain effects on other literacy, mathematics, social studies, and behavioral outcomes.

---

WWC Intervention Report on the eMINTS Comprehensive Program, a professional development program for grades 4–8 teachers offering structured PD, coaching, and support for integrating technology into the classroom. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 8 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general mathematics achievement (Tier 2) and no discernible effects on literacy achievement.

WWC Intervention Report on Dana Center Mathematics Pathways, a postsecondary program offering multiple math pathways aligned to programs of study, accelerated enrollment in credit-bearing college math, integrated student supports, and evidence-based curricula and pedagogy. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 3 eligible. Found positive effects (Tier 1) on both college progression and progression through developmental education coursework.

```yaml
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01

```

WWC Intervention Report on Bottom Line, an intensive advising program for high school students from low-income households covering college applications, financial aid, and institution selection, plus ongoing campus support for up to six years. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on college enrollment and college progression for low-income and first-generation college-going students (Tier 3).

### 78. Fast Track: Elementary School

```yaml

source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01

```

WWC Intervention Report on Fast Track: Elementary School, a comprehensive multi-component intervention including the PATHS curriculum, parent groups, social skills training, home visiting, peer-pairing, and academic tutoring to reduce conduct problems and improve academic, behavioral, and social outcomes. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on emotional/internal behavior, external behavior, reading achievement, and social outcomes for children with or at risk for emotional disturbance (Tier 3).

WWC Intervention Report on World of Words (WOW), a supplementary PreK curriculum developing vocabulary, concept knowledge, and content knowledge in science through intentional conversations and shared book readings centered on science topics. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on language development (Tier 1) across a diverse, predominantly low-income sample.

```yaml
description_inferred: false

```

WWC Intervention Report on Fast ForWord®, a computer-based reading program running 30–100 minutes daily for 4–16 weeks that develops the cognitive skills necessary for successful reading and learning in grades K–10. Reviews 8 studies meeting standards out of 29 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading fluency and comprehension, and no discernible effects on alphabetics or general literacy achievement for adolescent learners.

WWC Intervention Report on CW-FIT, a classroom management strategy for PreK–10 designed to create a positive learning environment by decreasing disruptive behavior and improving students' social skills through team-based incentive structures. Reviews 8 studies meeting standards out of 19 eligible. Found positive effects on student behavior (Tier 1) and positive effects on teacher practice (Tier 3).

### 93. First Step to Success

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/179"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, sel, school-discipline, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on First Step to Success, an early intervention program for grades K–3 at-risk children employing a trained behavior coach across three modules — screening, classroom intervention, and parent training — to prevent aggressive or antisocial behavioral patterns. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found positive effects on external behavior, potentially positive effects on emotional/internal behavior, social outcomes, and academic performance, and no discernible effects on reading achievement.

WWC Intervention Report on Reading Mastery, a systematic reading instruction program for grades K–6 available as an intervention for struggling readers, a supplement to core programs, or a standalone curriculum, with students grouped by reading level and continuous progress monitoring. Reviews 2 studies meeting standards out of 7 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on reading fluency and no discernible effects on comprehension for adolescent learners (Tier 3).

```yaml
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false

```

WWC Intervention Report on summer counseling programs delivered between high school graduation and college enrollment via text messaging, email, phone, in-person meetings, and social media to help students complete enrollment tasks and overcome barriers to college attendance. Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 6 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on credit accumulation and persistence, and mixed effects on college access and enrollment.

WWC Intervention Report on Core-Plus Mathematics, a 4-year high school curriculum replacing traditional course sequences with interwoven strands of algebra and functions, statistics and probability, geometry and trigonometry, and discrete mathematics emphasizing modeling, technology, and collaborative reasoning. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 10 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on mathematics achievement for high school students (Tier 3).

WWC Intervention Report on CIRC, a comprehensive reading and writing program for grades 2–8 incorporating story-related activities, direct reading comprehension instruction, and integrated language arts activities through cooperative pair and team learning strategies. WWC Intervention Report on phonological awareness training for PreK children with disabilities, a general practice teaching children to identify, detect, delete, segment, or blend segments of spoken words and to detect and produce rhyme or alliteration. WWC Intervention Report on Ladders to Literacy, a supplemental kindergarten early literacy curriculum organizing activities into three sections: print awareness, phonological awareness, and oral language skills. Reviews 4 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
```yaml
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
```


WWC Intervention Report on Pathway to Academic Success, a program training grades 6–12 teachers to improve English learners' reading and writing by incorporating cognitive strategies — goal setting, prior knowledge activation, questioning, predictions, and writing evaluation — into literacy instruction. Reviews 3 studies meeting standards out of 5 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on writing quality (Tier 2), writing conventions, and general literacy achievement for a predominantly Hispanic English learner population.

Reviews 5 studies meeting standards out of 11 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on general reading achievement, mixed effects on reading fluency, and no discernible effects on alphabetics or comprehension.


WWC Intervention Report on Bright Beginnings, an early childhood curriculum for at-risk preschoolers based partly on High/Scope and Creative Curriculum, emphasizing literacy skills through nine thematic units targeting cognitive, social, emotional, and physical development. Found potentially positive effects on print knowledge and no discernible effects on oral language, phonological processing, or mathematics (Tier 3).

### 111. National Guard Youth ChalleNGe Program

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/329"

source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [k-12, dropout-prevention, career-readiness, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on National Guard Youth ChalleNGe, a residential education and training program for youth ages 16–18 who have dropped out of or been expelled from high school, with a 22-week residential component including GED preparation and youth development followed by one year of structured mentoring. Reviews 1 study meeting standards out of 4 eligible. Found potentially positive effects on school completion (Tier 3).

---

### 112. Failure Free Reading

```yaml
url: "https://ies.ed.gov/ncee/wwc/InterventionReport/170"
type: report
source: "What Works Clearinghouse"
url_confirmed: true
description_inferred: false
date_added: 2026-05-01
tags: [literacy, k-12, response-to-intervention, learning-engineering, wwc]
```

WWC Intervention Report on Failure Free Reading, a language development program for students in kindergarten through grade 12 scoring in the bottom 15% on standardized tests who have not responded to conventional reading instruction, targeting vocabulary, fluency, word recognition, and comprehension. Reviews 1 study meeting standards. Found potentially positive effects on comprehension and no discernible effects on alphabetics or reading fluency (Tier 3).
